name: Mom's Jersey Fresh Tomato Soup
recipeIngredient: 6 red ripe Jersey tomatoes, peeled, cored, and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, sauté onions in butter over medium heat for about 5 minutes until they are tender and golden.
2. Add the chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, lemon rind strips, chicken stock, cooked crumbled bacon, dried thyme, dried basil, salt, and pepper to the pot. Stir to combine.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for about 15 minutes, or until the tomatoes are tender.
4. Remove the lemon rind strips with a slotted spoon and discard them.
5. Use an immersion blender to puree the soup in batches, ensuring not to fill the blender more than halfway to avoid overflow.
6. Strain the pureed soup through a fine-mesh sieve into another pot or bowl to remove any solids.
7. Return the strained liquid to the pot and reheat gently over low heat until warmed through before serving.
8. Garnish each serving with croutons, basil sprigs, and an additional pinch of salt and pepper if desired.
Source: Jane Camarota. This recipe won in the soup category of the "Jersey Fresh Cookoff".
